does: This paper introduces BeHonest, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating honesty in large language models across three core dimensions: knowledge boundary awareness (recognizing when models lack information), deceit avoidance (refusing to spread misinformation), and response consistency (maintaining coherent claims across conversations). The authors construct ten distinct scenarios and evaluate nine popular LLMs—spanning both closed-source and open-source models from different families—against these honesty criteria, providing systematic analysis of how current models perform on these critical alignment dimensions and identifying specific failure modes in honest behavior.
connects: This work builds on emerging research in LLM alignment and evaluation that has traditionally prioritized helpfulness and harmlessness but largely neglected honesty despite its fundamental importance. It responds to growing recognition that deceptive or misinformed outputs from increasingly capable models pose severe societal risks, complementing earlier work on factuality checking, hallucination detection, and model calibration. The paper extends evaluation frameworks from prior alignment benchmarks by introducing honesty-specific metrics and scenarios, filling a gap between general instruction-following evaluation and specialized probing of truthful behavior.
enables: BeHonest establishes a foundation for future work in honest AI systems, enabling researchers to systematically identify where models fail to maintain truthful behavior and to develop targeted interventions. The benchmark can support the development of training methods that explicitly optimize for honesty without sacrificing capability, and it provides a standardized evaluation framework for comparing honesty improvements across model families and scales. This work opens pathways toward better understanding the relationship between model scale, training procedures, and honest behavior, ultimately supporting safer deployment of increasingly powerful language models through reliable measurement of a critical alignment property.
